info.title: BaseGate API
info.description: BaseGate is a unified capability gateway that normalizes heterogeneous AI providers behind a single request/response lifecycle. It supports synchronous, asynchronous, and streaming execution modes with built-in billing, usage tracking, session management, and provider failover.

tags.name: Responses
tags.description: Create, poll, and cancel BaseGate responses. Supports sync, async, and streaming execution modes.

tags.name: Sessions
tags.description: Manage long-lived interactive sessions (e.g. browser sandboxes, coding environments).

tags.name: Usage
tags.description: Query aggregated usage metrics scoped to the authenticated tenant.

tags.name: Callbacks
tags.description: Inbound webhook endpoint for upstream providers to deliver asynchronous task results. No authentication required.

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Dashboard-facing administrative endpoints for listing responses, sessions, capabilities, usage stats, and managing projects.

post.description:
Submit a request to a model capability. The execution mode (sync, async, or stream) is controlled via `execution_options.mode`. Sync requests block until completion and return the full response. Async requests return immediately with status `accepted` and a `poll_url`. Stream requests deliver server-sent events.

Idempotency is supported via the `Idempotency-Key` header.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Client-supplied idempotency key. If a response with the same key already exists for this tenant, it is returned instead of creating a new one.

parameters.name: X-Project-Id
parameters.description: Optional project ID for multi-project tenants.

202.description: Async response accepted. Poll the `poll_url` to check progress.

409.description: Idempotency key conflict — a different request was already submitted with this key.

get.description: Retrieve the current state of a response. Use this to poll async responses until they reach a terminal status (succeeded, failed, canceled, expired).

post.description: Request cancellation of a non-terminal response. The response transitions to `canceled` status.

404.description: Response not found or already in terminal state.

post.description: Create a new long-lived session for interactive capabilities (e.g. browser sandbox, coding environment). The session is created asynchronously and transitions from `creating` to `active` once the provider confirms readiness.

get.description: Retrieve the current state of a session.

post.description: Send an action to an active session. Only one action can be in-flight at a time per session; concurrent submissions return 409 Conflict.

400.description: Invalid request or session is in a terminal state.

409.description: Session is busy processing another action.

post.description: Gracefully close an active session. The session transitions to `closed` status and releases provider resources.

get.description: Returns aggregated usage metrics from finalized usage records, scoped to the authenticated tenant. Results are grouped by model and time bucket. Optionally joins billing records to include cost data.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Filters records with `created_at >= start of that day (UTC)`.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date in ISO 8601 format (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Inclusive — filters records through the end of that day (UTC).

parameters.name: include_cost
parameters.description: Set to `true` to join billing records and include `total_cost` in results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum rows per page. Default 100, maximum 500.

limit.description: The limit applied to this query.

offset.description: The offset applied to this query.

post.description:
Webhook endpoint for upstream providers to deliver asynchronous task completion results. No API authentication is required; the provider verifies its own identity via the adapter's signature verification. The response_id in the URL must correspond to a valid response with an active attempt.

The request body format is provider-specific and parsed by the adapter's `ParseCallback` method.

parameters.name: response_id
parameters.description: The response ID that this callback relates to.

requestBody.description: Provider-specific callback payload. The format varies by upstream provider and is parsed by the adapter.

schema.description: Provider-specific callback body. Schema varies by provider.

200.description: Callback accepted and state machine updated.

400.description: Missing response_id, adapter does not support callbacks, or parse failure.

404.description: Response or active attempt not found.

502.description: Adapter not found for this attempt.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of BaseGate responses. Supports filtering by org, model, status, and time range.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of items per page. Maximum 100.

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: Filter by organization (tenant) ID. 0 or omitted returns all.

parameters.name: start_timestamp
parameters.description: Filter responses created at or after this Unix timestamp.

parameters.name: end_timestamp
parameters.description: Filter responses created at or before this Unix timestamp.

get.description: Returns the full detail of a single response, including all attempts, usage records, and billing records.

200.description: Response detail with attempts, usage, and billing.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of BaseGate sessions. Supports filtering by org, status, and time range.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of items per page. Maximum 100.

parameters.name: start_timestamp
parameters.description: Filter sessions created at or after this Unix timestamp.

parameters.name: end_timestamp
parameters.description: Filter sessions created at or before this Unix timestamp.

get.description: Returns the full detail of a single session including all session actions.
Returns a paginated list of registered BaseGate capabilities.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of items per page. Maximum 100.

get.description: Returns aggregate usage statistics for the BaseGate dashboard KPI cards: total requests, success/failure/running counts, total cost, total tokens, and adapter circuit breaker states.

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: Filter by organization (tenant) ID. 0 or omitted returns stats across all tenants.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of BaseGate projects.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of items per page. Maximum 100.

post.description: Create a new BaseGate project under an organization.

put.description: Update name, description, or status of an existing project.

delete.description: Permanently delete a project by its project ID.

TokenAuth.description: API token for relay (consumer-facing) routes. Passed as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

AdminAuth.description: Admin session token for dashboard (admin-facing) routes. Passed as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

model.description: The model or capability name to invoke (e.g. `gpt-4o`, `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`).

input.description: The input payload. Format varies by capability — typically a string prompt, an array of message objects, or a structured input object.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata. The `user_id` key is treated as an end-user tracking alias.

webhook_url.description: URL to receive webhook notifications for async responses.

BaseGateResponse.description: The standard response object returned by BaseGate.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the response was created.

model.description: The model that processed (or is processing) this request.

output.description: Output items. Present when status is `succeeded`.

poll_url.description: Relative URL to poll for updates. Present only when status is non-terminal (`accepted`, `queued`, `running`).

BGOutputItem.description: A single element in the response output array.

content.description: The output content. Type depends on the `type` field — a string for text, an object for structured types.

role.description: The role that produced this output.

BGError.description: Error information. Present when status is `failed`.

param.description: OpenAI-compatible parameter name that caused the error.

provider.description: The upstream provider that handled the request.

attempt_no.description: Which attempt number produced this result (1-based).

BGSessionActionRequest.description: Request body for executing a session action.

input.description: Action input payload. Format depends on the action type.

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for action retry.

BGSessionActionResponse.description: Response from a session action execution.

session_id.description: The session this action belongs to.

output.description: Action output. Format depends on the action type.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the session was created.

response_id.description: The parent response ID that created this session.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp when the session will expire.

idle_timeout_sec.description: Idle timeout in seconds. Session transitions to expired after this duration of inactivity.

last_action_at.description: Unix timestamp of the last action.

closed_at.description: Unix timestamp when the session was closed. Zero if still open.

RelayErrorResponse.description: Error response format used by relay (consumer-facing) endpoints.

time_bucket.description: Time bucket label (format depends on granularity: `YYYY-MM-DD`, `YYYY-MM-DD HH:00:00`, or `YYYY-MM`).

total_units.description: Sum of billable_units in this bucket.

total_input.description: Sum of input_units in this bucket.

total_output.description: Sum of output_units in this bucket.

total_cost.description: Sum of billing amount in this bucket. Only present when `include_cost=true`.

BgResponse.description: Database-level response record (used in admin endpoints).

api_key_id.description: API key ID used for this request.

status_version.description: Optimistic concurrency version for the status field.

active_attempt_id.description: FK to the currently active attempt.

finalized_at.description: Unix timestamp when the response reached a terminal state.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp of expiration (for async responses).

pricing_snapshot_json.description: Frozen pricing snapshot at invocation time (JSON string).

estimated_quota.description: Pre-authorized quota amount reserved at dispatch time.

BgResponseAttempt.description: A single provider dispatch attempt for a response.

adapter_name.description: Name of the provider adapter used.

poll_after_at.description: Unix timestamp after which this attempt should be polled.

poll_count.description: Number of times this attempt has been polled.

BgUsageRecord.description: A usage record tied to a response.

BgBillingRecord.description: A billing record tied to a response.

BgSession.description: Database-level session record (used in admin endpoints).

BgSessionAction.description: A single action executed within a session.

BgProject.description: A tenant project in the BaseGate identity model.

CreateProjectRequest.description: Request body for creating a project.

org_id.description: Organization (tenant) ID to own the project.

UpdateProjectRequest.description: Request body for updating a project. All fields are optional; only non-empty fields are updated.

AdminBgResponseDetail.description: Full response detail including attempts, usage, and billing records.

attempts.description: All dispatch attempts for this response.

AdminBgSessionDetail.description: Full session detail including all session actions.

actions.description: All actions executed in this session.

AdminBgUsageStats.description: Aggregate usage statistics for the BaseGate dashboard.

running_count.description: Number of responses in non-terminal states (accepted, queued, running).

total_cost.description: Sum of billing amounts across all billing records.

total_tokens.description: Sum of billable_units from usage records where billable_unit is `token`.

adapters.description: Current circuit breaker states of all registered adapters.

AdapterCircuitInfo.description: Circuit breaker status for a single adapter.

cooldown_remaining_sec.description: Seconds remaining in cooldown (only present when circuit is open).

AdminPageResponse_BgResponse.description: Paginated admin response for BgResponse items.

total.description: Total number of items matching the query.

AdminPageResponse_BgSession.description: Paginated admin response for BgSession items.

AdminPageResponse_BgCapability.description: Paginated admin response for BgCapability items.

AdminPageResponse_BgProject.description: Paginated admin response for BgProject items.
